---
title: ""
pagetitle: "Quan (Kyle) Zhang — Health Economist"
description: "Quan (Kyle) Zhang — health economist and data scientist at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa. Causal inference, machine learning, population aging, and health equity."
---

::: {.name-block}
![](assets/profile.jpg){.profile-photo}

<div class="name-text">
<h1 class="site-name">Quan (Kyle) Zhang</h1>
<p class="site-role">Health Economist · Data Scientist</p>
<p class="site-affil">University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa<br><a href="https://www.economics.hawaii.edu/">Economics</a> · <a href="https://uhero.hawaii.edu/">UHERO</a> · <a href="https://jabsom.hawaii.edu/">John A. Burns School of Medicine</a></p>
<p class="site-contact"><a href="mailto:quanz@hawaii.edu">quanz@hawaii.edu</a> <span class="dot">·</span> <a href="https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=lWeyFo4AAAAJ">Google Scholar</a> <span class="dot">·</span> <a href="https://orcid.org/0009-0009-2409-309X">ORCID</a> <span class="dot">·</span> <a href="https://github.com/QuanZhangkyle">GitHub</a> <span class="dot">·</span> <a href="https://www.linkedin.com/in/quanzhangkyle/">LinkedIn</a> <span class="dot">·</span> <a href="https://www.researchgate.net/profile/Quan-Zhang-96">ResearchGate</a> <span class="dot">·</span> <a href="https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/cf_dev/AbsByAuth.cfm?per_id=11462228">SSRN</a></p>
</div>
:::

::: {.bio}
I am a health economist and data scientist at the University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa,
trained in both economics (Quantitative Economics & Econometrics) and quantitative
health sciences at the John A. Burns School of Medicine. I combine causal inference
and applied econometrics with machine learning, SQL, and large-scale data
engineering to study population aging, frailty dynamics, health shocks and labor
supply, disaster health economics, and health equity — working with longitudinal
microdata such as CHARLS, HRS, and SHARE in R, Python, SQL, and Stata.

As a researcher at UHERO, I contribute to the NIH- and state-funded Maui Wildfire
Exposure Study (MauiWES). I am also an invited collaborator and peer
reviewer for the Global Burden of Disease Study 2023 at the Institute for Health
Metrics and Evaluation (IHME), University of Washington.
:::

## Methods & Tools

::: {.skills}
**Machine learning & data science** — gradient boosting (XGBoost), random forests, LASSO/elastic net, causal forests & heterogeneous treatment effects, double/debiased ML (DML), survival models, multiple imputation (MICE), etc.

**Causal inference & econometrics** — difference-in-differences (DiD, incl. staggered adoption), instrumental variables (IV/2SLS), event-study & panel methods, regression discontinuity (RDD), synthetic control, etc.

**Biostatistics & bioinformatics** — survival analysis (Cox PH, Kaplan–Meier, AFT), mixed-effects & longitudinal models, biomarker and genotype–phenotype analysis, frailty-index construction, IRT/CFA psychometrics, cost-effectiveness analysis (CEA/DCEA), meta-analysis, etc.

**Programming & software** — R, Python, SQL, Stata; Git, Bash, LaTeX; high-performance computing (HPC); reproducible data pipelines over CHARLS, HRS, SHARE, NHANES, IPUMS, GBD, etc.
:::

## Research Interests

- Health economics and policy
- Causal inference and applied econometrics for health and social outcomes
- Population aging, frailty dynamics, and health and labor supply over the life course
- Disaster health economics and community resilience
- Health equity and quantitative health sciences

## Education

- **PhD in Economics** (Quantitative Economics & Econometrics), University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa
- **Quantitative Health Sciences**, John A. Burns School of Medicine, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa

## Awards & Honors

- **AWARD Network Summer Institute**, University of California, San Francisco — a highly competitive national selection (≈20 scholars chosen nationwide; NIA/NIH-funded), 2026
- **Bernie Campbell Fellowship Fund Award**, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, 2026
- **Research Stipend**, College of Social Sciences (Department of Economics), University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, 2026
- **Burnham O. Campbell Fund Award**, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, 2025
- **Hung Family Scholarship Award**, 2025
- **Hung Family Scholarship Award**, 2024
- **Jordan & Cara Odo Foundation Leadership Scholarship**, 2024
- **Achievement Award**, Department of Economics, University of Hawaiʻi at Mānoa, 2023

## News

::: {.news-list}
::: {.news-item}
[2026]{.news-date} New paper in *Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS)*: "Do Negative Social Ties Accelerate Aging in Adults, or Does Aging Erode Social Ties?"
:::

::: {.news-item}
[2026]{.news-date} Selected for the **AWARD Network Summer Institute** (UCSF) — a highly competitive, NIA/NIH-funded national program (≈20 scholars selected nationwide).
:::

::: {.news-item}
[Nov 2026]{.news-date} Paper accepted at the **Southern Economic Association (SEA) 96th Annual Meeting**, Houston, TX.
:::
:::

## Selected Publications

::: {.pub-list}
::: {.pub}
Zhang, Q. (2026). Do Negative Social Ties Accelerate Aging in Adults, or Does Aging Erode Social Ties? *Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences (PNAS)*, 123(20), e2608036123.

[<i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i> DOI](https://doi.org/10.1073/pnas.2608036123){.pub-link} [<i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i> Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=lWeyFo4AAAAJ){.pub-link}
:::

::: {.pub}
Zhang, Q. (2025). Overcoming bias in a fall prevention program in resource-limited areas. *JAMA*.

[<i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i> DOI](https://doi.org/10.1001/jama.2025.21041){.pub-link} [<i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i> Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=lWeyFo4AAAAJ){.pub-link}
:::

::: {.pub}
Zhang, Q. (2026). Keep REMS focused on safety, not as a barrier to generics. *Health Affairs*.

[<i class="bi bi-box-arrow-up-right"></i> DOI](https://doi.org/10.1377/hlthaff.2025.01565){.pub-link} [<i class="bi bi-mortarboard-fill"></i> Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=lWeyFo4AAAAJ){.pub-link}
:::
:::

[See all publications →](publications.qmd){.more-link}
